2605.17596.title: NeuSymMS: A Hybrid Neuro-Symbolic Memory System for Persistent, Self-Curating LLM Agents
2605.17596.paper_summary: We present NeuSymMS, an adaptive memory system that enables large language model (LLM) agents to learn, remember, and reason about users across sessions via a hybrid neuro-symbolic architecture. NeuSymMS couples neural fact extraction from unstructured dialogue with a CLIPS-based expert system that classifies, deduplicates, and reconciles facts under explicit lifecycle rules. The system represents knowledge as subject-relation-value triples stored in relational database management system, supports user/agents/agent-to-agents scoping, and implements a dual-horizon short-term/long-term memory model with access-based promotion and time-based pruning. NeuSymMS maintains continuity of memory while avoiding context-window bloat and cross-entity contamination. We argue that this architecture offers a practical path to trustworthy, auditable memory for production agentic systems and discuss its novelty relative to log retrieval, summarization, and key-value approaches.
2605.17596.paper_summary_zh: 我們提出了NeuSymMS，一個自適應記憶系統，使大型語言模型（LLM）代理能夠通過混合神經-符號架構在會話之間學習、記住和推理有關用戶的資訊。NeuSymMS將從非結構化對話中提取的神經事實與基於CLIPS的專家系統相結合，該系統根據明確的生命周期規則對事實進行分類、去重和調和。該系統將知識表示為存儲在關聯數據庫管理系統中的主題-關係-值三元組，支持用戶/代理/代理之間的範圍，並實現了一個基於訪問促進和基於時間修剪的雙視野短期/長期記憶模型。NeuSymMS在避免上下文窗口膨脹和跨實體污染的同時保持記憶的連續性。我們認為這種架構為生產代理系統提供了一條實用的可信、可審計記憶的途徑，並討論了相對於日誌檢索、摘要和鍵值方法的創新性。
2605.17596.author: Mujahid Sultan et.al.
2605.17596.authors: Mujahid Sultan, Sri Thuraisamy, Daya Rajaratnam
